chunks.text: HIGH COURT OF SINDH, CIRCUIT COURT HYDERABAD Crl. Misc: Application No.S-146 of 2026 DATE ORDER WITH SIGNATURE OF JUDGE(S) 1. For orders on office objection (s) 2. For hearing of main case 16.04.2026 Mr.Pervaiz Tariq Tagar, Advocate for the applicant Mr.Siraj Ahmed Bijarani, Assistant P.G for the State a/w SIP Muhammad Paryal of PS Qazi Ahmed ********* This Criminal Miscellaneous Application has been filed by Iqbal Chandio son of Arab Chandio, attaching copy of the order dated 12.02.2026 passed by the learned Additional Sessions Judge-IV, Shaheed Benazirabad. As per the said order dated 12.02.2026, it appears that one Muhammad son of Arab Chandio sought recovery of the fiancé of his brother alleged detainee Mst.Azeema. The order records that the detainee appeared before the concerned court and left the court with her maternal uncle (mamoo) Shabbir Ahmed Chandio. Now Iqbal Chandio (and not Muhammad) claims production of the detainee. Learned counsel for the applicant candidly concedes that the present applicant Iqbal Chandio is the brother of the said Muhammad son of Arab Chandio, but has/had not moved the application under Section 491 Cr.P.C, when the detainee had appeared before the Court. Now, Iqbal has moved this application without exhausting the remedy before the concerned Sessions Court instead he has directly approached the High Court. The detainee is an adult. She was in attendance before the Sessions Court two months ago when the present applicants’ brother filed an application under Section 491 Cr.P.C before the concerned Sessions Court. As an adult the detainee is at liberty to decide her movement and abode.

She was in attendance before the Sessions Court two months ago when the present applicants’ brother filed an application under Section 491 Cr.P.C before the concerned Sessions Court. As an adult the detainee is at liberty to decide her movement and abode. This application moved by her alleged husband without approaching the concerned Sessions Court seeking her production is misconceived. Accordingly, the application is dismissed for the above reasons. As per 2 of 2 the case pleaded by his counsel today, Iqbal Chandio may file application before the competent forum to address his grievance concerning co- habitation, subject to all legal consequences. JUDGE AHSAN K. ABRO
